title: Hire a temporary foreign worker with a Labour Market Impact Assessment - Canada.ca
content:
## Hire a temporary foreign worker with a Labour Market Impact Assessment
Determine which type of Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) application you should submit to hire a temporary foreign worker. Understand your responsibilities when hiring under the Temporary Foreign Worker Program.

## Ebola disease: Temporary measures
For employers with workers arriving from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), Uganda and South Sudan, consult Ebola disease: Temporary measures to learn to more on the Government of Canada’s temporary measures in response to the Ebola disease.

## Changes to the Temporary Foreign Worker Program for low-wage positions
Recent changes have been made to the Temporary Foreign Worker Program:
- As of April 1, 2026, employers submitting a Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) application for low-wage positions must: advertise the job offer for a minimum of 8 consecutive weeks in the 3 months before submitting the application, and target youth in recruitment efforts
- advertise the job offer for a minimum of 8 consecutive weeks in the 3 months before submitting the application, and
- target youth in recruitment efforts
- As early as April 1, 2026, employers in rural areas may be eligible for temporary measures on the proportion of temporary foreign workers hired for some low-wage positions located in regions outside census metropolitan areas and within participating provinces and territories
- Find out if you need an LMIA
- LMIA application processing times
- Employer Contact Centre
- Processing centres contact information
- Are you a worker? Apply to work in Canada

## Determine what stream to apply for

### High and low-wage positions
Hire a temporary foreign worker for a wide range of positions based on the provincial or territorial wage threshold.

### Primary agriculture positions
Hire an agricultural temporary foreign worker in occupations and activities related to primary agriculture.

### Applications to support permanent residency
Hire a skilled temporary foreign worker to support their permanent residence through an immigration program.

### Global Talent Stream
Hire uniquely skilled talent or in-demand workers to scale up and grow your business.
Hire a temporary foreign worker to care for children, seniors or persons with medical needs.

### Foreign academic positions
Hire a foreign academic at a degree-granting post-secondary educational institution in Canada.

### Hiring in the province of Quebec
Hire temporary foreign workers for positions in Quebec via regular or facilitated processes.

### Recognized Employer Pilot
Hire temporary foreign workers through a simplified online application process.

### Refusal to process applications
Reasons why the Temporary Foreign Worker Program refuses to process certain applications.

### Employer compliance obligations
How to comply with your obligations under the Temporary Foreign Worker Program.

### Modification to a positive LMIA
Take appropriate steps to make a change to a positive LMIA.

### Employer voluntary disclosure of non-compliance
Voluntarily tell us about possible compliance issues before an inspection is launched.

### List of non-compliant employers
Employers found to be non-compliant during an inspection.
Tell us if you suspect a temporary foreign worker is being abused.

### Apply via LMIA Online
LMIA Online is reliable and secure. Use it to submit an application to hire a temporary foreign worker. A Job Bank account is required.
- Employment and Social Development Canada
- Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
